title: SQLiteStorageProvider silently drops records for tables/columns not in schema.sql
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

store_record -> _generate_insert builds 'INSERT INTO <table>' dynamically, but tables are only created from schema.sql at init (_create_tables). A record for an undefined table/column raises OperationalError, which store_record CATCHES and logs as 'Unsupported record' -- the record is silently DROPPED, no table created. For a measurement framework this masks data loss as a log line. Contrast: MemoryStructuredProvider.store_record appends to a defaultdict keyed by table, accepting any table dynamically (used by PR #1154's stealth detection-results refactor). Options: (a) make the drop loud/counted/surfaced at crawl end, (b) raise a clear error specifically for 'no such table' (programmer error) while tolerating transient per-record issues, or (c) restore dynamic table creation.
